danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile creature sprites in overworld and dungeons
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats to Avatar and party; world corruption/danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites attacking; particle effects or weapon animations
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger requiring active response; no turn-based safety

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, multi-layered underground or shadowed tile-sets
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk areas; locations of significant threats and plot dangers

danger_signals.visual_form: Characters drawing weapons or fleeing dialogue
danger_signals.meaning: Shift from safety to hostility; social danger or deception revealed

safety_signals.visual_form: Structured building clusters, NPCs in stationary positions
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled spaces; reduced monster spawning; faction presence
Stable world-state; opportunity for crafting, trading, puzzle-solving

safety_signals.visual_form: Guard NPCs, royal emblems in buildings, structured militia presence
safety_signals.meaning: Order maintained; legitimate governance active in region

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC interaction prompts; keyword options displayed
safety_signals.meaning: Stable social interaction; no immediate combat; information gathering possible

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies undermining crown authority appear hostile to guards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Guard barracks, royal chambers, structured military bases

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Deceptive NPCs initially appearing friendly; reveal as threats during dialogue
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Shadowed meeting places, hidden lairs associated with schemes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct creature sprites; hostile attack animations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Infested zones, dungeons, wilderness areas lacking civilization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster sprite density increases; darker tile coloration; environmental decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Creatures actively spawn and attack; NPCs may flee or express fear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard presence in towns; structured NPC positions; well-maintained building states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs follow routines; guards patrol; dialogue trees accessible and stable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPCs appear normal but dialogue reveals hidden agendas; visual disguise of intent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters interact peacefully until dialogue reveals conspiracy; sudden behavioral shifts possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprites move toward party; combat particle effects; weapon animations active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All party members engaged in autonomous combat; AI makes tactical positioning changes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stackable objects visible; workstations (forges, cooking areas) rendered; container gumps open
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game pauses during inventory/crafting actions; objects respond to drag-and-drop interaction

environmental_storytelling.example: Weapons, tools, and items scattered in dungeons tell stories of previous battles or occupants; stacking items reveals puzzle solutions and world traversal history
Guards stationed at specific locations communicate authority presence; NPCs in homes or workplaces indicate civilization stability
Monster-infested areas show visual corruption compared to orderly towns; darker palettes and hostile sprite presence indicate danger zones
Keywords only appear when encountered in plot; conversation options reveal world lore and political situations as Avatar discovers them
Stackable objects and vertical space convey dungeon complexity and environmental puzzle design; inaccessible areas until items form stairs indicate progression barriers
Books, scrolls, and equipment displays show character history; what NPCs carry or store hints at their role and affiliation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Mouse-driven movement; drag-and-drop interaction with objects; inventory management via gump interface
Real-time positioning changes; automatic party AI engagement; optional manual targeting for Avatar; pause only when accessing inventory mid-action
Follow Avatar; remain in formation; respond to dialogue and interaction prompts
Autonomous AI behavior execution; weapon switching based on tactical assessment; retreat from overwhelming threats; occasional self-defeating moves (e.g., friendly fire)
Fixed or patrol-route positioning; dialogue accessible; represent crown authority and order
Turn hostile toward monsters/conspirators; engage in combat; movement toward danger sources
Appear as normal townspeople; friendly dialogue initially; reveal hostility through specific keyword triggers or plot progression
Draw weapons; change dialogue tone; may attack or flee depending on tactical assessment
Stationary or slow patrol; become aggressive when Avatar approaches
Attack with sprite-based animations; move toward target; no dialogue or negotiation possible

notes: Ultima VII's visual communication relies heavily on real-time interaction dynamics and environmental contrast. The shift from tile-based grid movement to continuous space allows for fluid danger/safety signaling through NPC proximity and environmental density. Factions are communicated primarily through NPC role-positioning (guards vs. civilians) and dialogue trees rather than distinct visual markers. The game's paradigm emphasizes world interactivity (draggable objects, stackable puzzles, multi-layered maps) as a manifestation system — players read world-state through what can be manipulated and what resists interaction. Deception is a core political mechanic: conspirators appear visually identical to legitimate NPCs until dialogue keywords unlock their true allegiances. The Famicom port (1994) would compress these visual systems but retains core interaction paradigms.
